title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. subglaucum
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. subglaucum, also known as a subspecies of the common daisy, is a flowering plant native to Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. subglaucum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote a healthy immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for various health benefits.
